title: CIL Industrial Engineering — Advanced Mock 13 (Conceptual)
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.stem: A cylindrical tank 7 m deep has diameter 14 m. Time to fill at 11 kL/min:
questions.solution: 9.8 min

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average weight of 8 persons increases by 2.5 kg when a new person replaces one weighing 65 kg. The new person's weight is:
questions.solution: 85 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If a:b = 2:3 and b:c = 3:5, find a:b:c:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Find the area of a rhombus with diagonals 12 cm and 16 cm:
questions.solution: 96 cm²

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A train passes a pole in 12 sec and a platform 120 m in 24 sec. Length:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Fresh grapes contain 80% water, dry grapes 20% water. Dry grapes from 120 kg fresh:
questions.solution: 30 kg

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Several states have announced drought relief packages. Conclusion I: These states are facing drought. II: Central government will also announce relief.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All papers are files. Some files are folders. All folders are covers. Conclusions: I. Some covers are files. II. Some folders are papers.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a 5×5 square grid, how many squares?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man faces west. Turns 135° clockwise, then 45° anticlockwise. Which direction now?
questions.solution: South

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: AY, BW, CU, DS, ?
questions.solution: EQ

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Scores: P > Q, R < S, Q > S, T > P. Who scores highest?
questions.solution: T

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: India's coal imports fell 20% this year compared to last year. Conclusion I: Domestic coal production has increased. II: Coal consumption has decreased.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All fruits are sweet. Some sweet are sour. No sour is bitter. Conclusions: I. Some fruits are sour. II. Some sweet are not bitter.
questions.solution: Only II follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a 4×4 dot grid, how many squares can be formed?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Sita walks 30 m north, 40 m east. Geeta walks 40 m south, 30 m east from same point. Distance between them:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the missing: AD, GJ, MP, ?
questions.solution: SV

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Five boxes stacked. Box 1 below Box 3. Box 4 above Box 1. Box 2 below Box 5. Box 3 above Box 5. Which is bottommost?
questions.solution: Box 2

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statement: Coal production has grown 8% this year while renewable energy grew 25%. Conclusion I: India is moving towards green energy. II: Coal production is declining in absolute terms.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All coins are notes. No note is currency. All currency is paper. Conclusions: I. No coin is paper. II. Some notes are not paper.
questions.solution: Neither follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Two concentric circles with radii 5 and 3. How many tangents can be drawn to both circles?
questions.solution: 2 direct + 2 transverse

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A walks 25 m north, 15 m east, 25 m south. Another man B walks 15 m west from same start. B is in which direction of A?
questions.solution: West

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A transport company must send 8 trucks. 3 are needed for emergency. Only 5 are available. How many combinations possible?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Should mining companies be mandated to spend 5% of profit on local community development? Arg I: Yes, mining affects local communities. Arg II: No, it reduces profitability.
questions.solution: Only I is strong

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A walks 30 m north, turns right 40 m, right 50 m, right 40 m. Distance from start:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A points to a photo and says 'He is the son of the only daughter of my father-in-law'. How is the person related to A?
questions.solution: Son

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 2, 5, 10, 17, 26, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: Some apples are oranges. No orange is grape. All grapes are sweet. Conclusions: I. Some apples are not grapes. II. Some sweets are not oranges.
questions.solution: Both follow

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong term: 2, 5, 10, 17, 28, 37, 50

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a code, 512 means 'you are good', 231 means 'they are bad', 145 means 'good and bad'. Code for 'they' is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next: 2, 4, 16, 256, ?

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / the / demanded / conditions / working / better
options: Demanded the workers better working conditions
Working conditions demanded the workers better
The workers demanded better working conditions
Better working conditions the workers demanded
questions.solution: The workers demanded better working conditions

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The committee have submitted its report.
options: The committee have submitted its report.
The committee has submitted their report.
The committee has submitted its report.
The committee have submitted their report.
questions.solution: The committee has submitted its report.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: plays / role / coal / in / energy / a / vital / production / India
options: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production
Vital role coal plays in energy production India
In India coal plays a energy vital production role
Coal in India plays vital role energy production
questions.solution: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is the tallest of the two sisters.
options: She is the taller of the two sisters.
She is the tallest of the two sisters.
She is more tall of the two sisters.
She is tall among the two sisters.
questions.solution: She is the taller of the two sisters.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: sustainable / practices / mining / promote / must
options: Sustainable development must promote mining practices
Mining practices must promote sustainable development
Must promote sustainable development mining practices
Practices mining sustainable must promote development
questions.solution: Mining practices must promote sustainable development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: Hardly had he arrived than the meeting started.
options: He had hardly arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived then the meeting started.
Hardly he had arrived than the meeting started.
Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.
questions.solution: Hardly had he arrived when the meeting started.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: country / infrastructure / of / development / backbone / the / is
options: The backbone of country development is infrastructure
Development of country backbone is infrastructure
Country development backbone is the infrastructure
Infrastructure is the backbone of country development
questions.solution: Infrastructure is the backbone of country development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: The furniture in this room are expensive.
options: The furniture in this room are expensive.
The furnitures in this room are expensive.
The furniture in this room is expensive.
The furniture in this room were expensive.
questions.solution: The furniture in this room is expensive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: data / protect / essential / is / privacy / to
options: Essential is to protect data privacy
Data privacy is essential to protect
questions.solution: Protect data privacy is essential

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more smarter than his brother.
options: He is more smarter than his brother.
He is smart than his brother.
He is smartest than his brother.
He is smarter than his brother.
questions.solution: He is smarter than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: renewable / future / energy / is / the
questions.solution: Renewable energy is the future

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is more cleverer than his brother.
options: He is more clever than his brother.
He is clever than his brother.
He is more cleverer than his brother.
He is cleverer than his brother.
questions.solution: He is cleverer than his brother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: environmental / concerns / need / planning / careful
questions.solution: Environmental concerns need careful planning

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: He is suffering with high fever.
options: He is suffering from high fever.
He is suffering of high fever.
He is suffering by high fever.
He is suffering with high fever.
questions.solution: He is suffering from high fever.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: mining / India / in / industry / is / a / important / sector
options: An important sector in India is mining industry
Mining industry is a important sector in India
Mining is an important sector in India
In India mining is important sector
questions.solution: Mining is an important sector in India

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
options: She is one of the most talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the talented singer in the industry.
She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.
She is the most talented singer in the industry.
questions.solution: She is one of the most talented singers in the industry.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: government / new / the / announced / policy / has / a / mining
options: The government has a new mining policy announced
Government has the new mining policy announced
The government has announced a new mining policy
Announced a new mining policy the government has
questions.solution: The government has announced a new mining policy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of COPIOUS:
questions.solution: Abundant

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: P: Artificial intelligence is transforming industries. Q: Machine learning algorithms analyse vast amounts of data. R: AI-powered systems can make predictions and recommendations. S: Ethical considerations around AI are being debated.
questions.solution: PQRS

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: She ____ a keen interest in geology.
questions.solution: showed

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PRUDENT:
questions.solution: Cautious

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The evidence ____ against the accused.
questions.solution: testified

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of AMICABLE:
questions.solution: Friendly

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ABSTAIN:
questions.solution: Indulge

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of OBSTINATE:
questions.solution: Stubborn

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Cellular manufacturing groups:
options: Machines and processes for a product family together
questions.solution: Cell: U-shaped layout. Machines in process sequence for family. Reduces transport, WIP, lead time.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Concept testing in product development:
options: Evaluates customer response before full development
questions.solution: Concept test: present concept to target market. Gauge interest, purchase intent. Low cost to iterate early.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Multiple activity chart:
options: Simultaneous activities of man + machine. Identify idle time. Gang chart
questions.solution: Multiple activity: man-machine ratio. Gang chart: team activities. Balance workload.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Simulated annealing: temperature schedule determines:
questions.solution: SA: accept worse solution with P = exp(-ΔE/T). T decreases over time. Allows escaping local optima.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory turnover vs days of supply:
options: Turnover = COGS / average inventory. Days = 365 / turnover. High turnover = efficient. Low = overstock
questions.solution: Inventory turnover: varies by industry (grocery 12-15, auto 4-6, jewelery 1-2).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: VE: function definition format is verb + noun:
options: Examples: transmit electricity, store energy, support load
questions.solution: Function: action + object. Measurable. Example: convert energy, transmit signal, support weight.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Warehouse layout: U-flow design provides:
options: Single dock for receiving and shipping
questions.solution: U-flow: receiving and shipping on same side. Efficient use of dock equipment. Good for cross-docking.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Capacity planning:
options: Determines production capacity to meet demand. RCCP, CRP
questions.solution: Capacity: theoretical vs demonstrated. Rough-cut (RCCP) for MPS. CRP for MRP.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Reliability block diagram: series system reliability is:
questions.solution: Series: R = R₁ × R₂ × ... × Rn. Less than smallest. Adding components decreases system reliability in series.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Improve phase tools:
options: DOE, Poka-yoke, Kaizen, simulation. Solution generation and selection
questions.solution: Improve: generate solutions. Piloting. Design of Experiments (DOE). Response surface methods.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ergonomics — metabolic energy expenditure:
options: Energy cost of work: VO₂ (ml/kg/min). 1 MET = 3.5 ml O₂/kg/min. Light < 3 MET, Heavy > 6 MET
questions.solution: Metabolic: oxygen consumption. Energy (kcal/min) = VO₂ × 5 (kcal/L O₂). Work-rest cycle.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Genetic algorithm (GA):
options: Selection (tournament, roulette), crossover (PMX, OX), mutation (swap, invert). Population evolves. Elitism
questions.solution: GA: binary or permutation encoding. Fitness proportionate selection. Premature convergence. Diversity.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Method study focuses on:
options: Finding the most economical way of doing job
questions.solution: Method study: systematic analysis of methods.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Variance reduction: antithetic variates technique uses:
questions.solution: Antithetic: if U is random, use 1-U as companion. Negative correlation reduces variance of mean estimate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principles of motion economy: gravity feed bins:
options: Parts delivered by gravity to point of use (tilted bins, chutes)
questions.solution: Gravity feed: parts slide to point of use. Reduces reach and search. Close to assembly point.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Goal programming: objective is to:
questions.solution: Goal programming: minimize weighted sum of deviations from targets. Multiple conflicting goals. Satisficing approach.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: LP — unbounded solution:
options: Objective unlimited. Feasible region open. No finite optimum
questions.solution: Unbounded: missing constraint. Check entering variable has no positive ratio.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Bullwhip effect amplification is caused by:
options: Demand forecasting updating and order batching
questions.solution: Bullwhip: demand variability increases upstream.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CUSUM (Cumulative Sum) control chart:
options: C_t = max(0, C_{t-1} + x_t - μ_0 - k). Decision interval H. Designed for small shifts detection
questions.solution: CUSUM: V-mask. k = half shift (1σ). H = 4-5σ. ARL profile. Better than Shewhart for Δ < 1.5σ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An assembly line has total task time 40 min, 5 stations and a cycle time of 12 min. Its balance efficiency is:

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Scope creep prevention requires:
questions.solution: Change control: documented requests, impact analysis, approval process. Baseline prevents unauthorized scope expansion.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CORELAP (Computerized Relationship Layout):
options: Constructive. Total closeness rating (TCR). Highest TCR placed first
questions.solution: CORELAP: TCR = Σ closeness ratings. Place to maximize adjacency score. Rectilinear.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Six Sigma DMAIC: Control phase uses:
questions.solution: Control: maintain gains. Use SPC charts, control plans, visual management. Prevent backsliding.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹20000, price ₹120, variable cost ₹70/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹10000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Root cause analysis: fault tree analysis (FTA):
options: Top-down, deductive. Uses AND/OR gates to link events
questions.solution: FTA: top event -> causes -> basic events. AND gate: all inputs needed. OR gate: any input sufficient.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Forecast — collaborative:
options: Sales + marketing + supply chain consensus. Combines multiple perspectives
questions.solution: Collaborative: improves buy-in. Combines statistical + judgmental. Adjust for promotions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shortest path: Dijkstra's algorithm requires:
questions.solution: Dijkstra: non-negative. Bellman-Ford: allows negative (no negative cycles). A*: heuristic.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: Postponement strategy: final product configuration delayed until:
questions.solution: Postponement: delay differentiation. Reduces inventory, increases flexibility. Dell model: build to order.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Value engineering: Value = Function / Cost. To increase value:
options: Increase function or decrease cost (or both)
questions.solution: VE: improve value per unit cost. Function analysis identifies what customer pays for.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: A3 problem solving uses:
questions.solution: A3: Toyota. One page captures problem, analysis, countermeasures, follow-up. PDCA on one page.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: P-Q analysis:
options: Pareto on product volume vs variety. X: high V, low P → product layout. Y: medium → cellular. Z: low V, high P → process
questions.solution: P-Q: product-quantity curve. Above break-even: product layout. Below: process layout.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lot-for-lot ordering in MRP:
options: Order exactly what is needed for each period (no inventory carryover)
questions.solution: L4L: order quantity = net requirement. Minimizes holding. More setups.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Supply chain: lead time = processing + transit + waiting + inspection:
options: Total time from order to delivery
questions.solution: Lead time: sum of all delays. Reducing each component improves responsiveness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spare parts classification — VED:
options: Vital (stockout → shutdown), Essential (delayed), Desirable (convenience). Combine with ABC for priority
questions.solution: VED: maintenance criticality. Vital: stockout cost > holding cost. Stock at warehouse.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RCM (Reliability-Centered Maintenance):
options: Systematic method to determine maintenance strategy. Functions, failures, consequences
questions.solution: RCM: 7 questions. 4 failure consequences: hidden, safety, environmental, operational. Proactive tasks.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Reliability growth: Duane model:
options: Cumulative MTBF increases with cumulative failures: MTBF = a·N^b (b>0)
questions.solution: Duane: learning curve. b>0: reliability improving. b=0: constant. b<0: reliability degrading.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF):
options: Total operating time / Number of failures
questions.solution: MTBF: average time between failures. Higher = more reliable. MTTR: average repair time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bath tub curve:
options: Early failure (infant mortality) → Random (constant) → Wear-out (increasing). Equipment life
questions.solution: Bath tub: three phases. Infant mortality: manufacturing defects. Wear-out: age-related deterioration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vendor managed inventory (VMI):
options: Supplier monitors buyer's inventory and replenishes independently
questions.solution: VMI: vendor decides order quantities. Reduces bullwhip. Needs trust and data sharing.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost = ₹10000, price = ₹80/unit, variable cost = ₹60/unit. The break-even quantity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Queuing — M/G/1:
options: General service distribution. Pollaczek-Khinchin formula. L = λ·E[S] + λ²·E[S²]/(2(1-ρ))
questions.solution: P-K: L = ρ + (λ²·σ²+ρ²)/(2(1-ρ)). For M/M/1: E[S²] = 2/μ² (exponential).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Principles of motion economy:
options: Use of body, arrangement of workplace, design of tools/equipment
questions.solution: Motion economy: both hands work together, smooth continuous motions, gravity feed, drop delivery.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed cost ₹30000, price ₹100, variable cost ₹60/unit. The output needed for a target profit of ₹10000 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Correlation vs regression:
options: Correlation: strength of relationship (r, -1 to 1). Regression: model to predict Y from X (b coefficient)
questions.solution: r: Pearson's. r²: coefficient of determination. Simple regression: Y = β₀ + β₁X. Multiple: Y = β₀ + ΣβᵢXᵢ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a process (functional) layout, similar machines are grouped:
questions.solution: Process layout suits low-volume, high-variety (job-shop) production.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seasonal decomposition: multiplicative model:
options: Y = T * S * C * I (Trend x Seasonal x Cyclical x Irregular)
questions.solution: Multiplicative: seasonal component proportional to trend. Additive: seasonal constant regardless of trend.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cross-docking — types:
options: Pre-distribution (labeled at source) vs post-distribution (sort at DC). Flow-through rate >30%
questions.solution: Cross-docking: Wal-Mart pioneered. Flow-through: % cross-docked. Requires advanced WMS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 2000 units, ordering cost = ₹50/order, holding cost = ₹4/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 2 and tp = 16 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Make or buy decision:
options: Cost-based (variable cost, fixed cost) vs strategic (core competency, capacity)
questions.solution: Break-even: buy if volume < BEQ, make if > BEQ. Strategic: protect IP.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ABC analysis:
questions.solution: ABC: Pareto principle. A items: tight control, frequent review. C items: simple controls.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Performance rating: Westinghouse system rates:
options: Skill, effort, conditions, consistency (each rated A,B,C,D)
questions.solution: Westinghouse: 4 factors. Each A (best) to D (worst). Rating factor = average of 4 ratings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At the Economic Order Quantity, the annual ordering cost equals the:
questions.solution: EOQ is where the rising holding-cost curve crosses the falling ordering-cost curve — the two are equal.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A task has a normal time of 4 min with 10% allowances. Its standard time is:
questions.solution: Standard time = normal × (1 + allowance) = 4×(1 + 10/100) = 4.4 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: ABC analysis: C items (50-70% of items, 5-10% of value) are managed with:
options: Simple controls: two-bin system, periodic review
questions.solution: C items: many, low value. Simple methods: visual review, bulk ordering. Minimal investment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopwatch time study: continuous vs snapback:
options: Continuous: clock runs non-stop. Snapback: reset after each element
questions.solution: Continuous: record cumulative times. Snapback: record element times directly. Continuous preferred.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about EOQ is INCORRECT?
options: EOQ varies with the square root of demand
EOQ increases in direct proportion to annual demand
At EOQ ordering cost equals carrying cost
questions.solution: EOQ ∝ √D, not D; doubling demand raises EOQ only by √2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Simo chart:
options: Simultaneous motion chart. Two-handed with micro-motion time scale (TMU)
questions.solution: Simo: from film analysis. Therbligs on both hands. Used for MTM analysis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 2000/yr, order cost ₹40 and order quantity 400, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (2000/400)×40 = ₹200.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Standard time:
options: Normal time + allowances. Time for qualified worker to complete task at defined performance
questions.solution: Standard time = basic time + relaxation + contingency + policy allowances.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 5000 units, ordering cost = ₹100/order, holding cost = ₹4/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flow diagram:
options: Scale layout showing movement paths. Identify backtracking, congestion
questions.solution: Flow diagram: string diagram for multiple trips. Distance traveled measured.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about control charts is correct?
options: Control limits equal the specification limits
Points within limits always mean zero defects
Control limits are based on process variation, not on specifications
questions.solution: Control limits (±3σ) come from the process itself; specification limits come from the customer — they are different.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CONWIP (Constant WIP):
options: Pull system with constant WIP level. Card count = max WIP. More flexible than kanban. Exponential weighting
questions.solution: CONWIP: cards released when job finished. WIP cap. Lower WIP → shorter cycle time (Little's law).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work sampling:
options: Random observations → % of time spent on activities. Statistical (confidence level)
questions.solution: Work sampling: ratio delay. Non-intrusive. Sample size for ±5% accuracy at 95% confidence.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With α = 0.1, last actual = 300 and last forecast = 220, the next exponential-smoothing forecast is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about exponential smoothing is correct?
options: A higher α makes the forecast react faster to recent data
A higher α makes it smoother
α has no effect on responsiveness
questions.solution: α ∈ (0,1); larger α weights the latest actual more, increasing responsiveness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For the CPM network shown (path 1-2-4 = A+B = 5+6, path 1-3-4 = C+D = 2+9), the project duration (critical path) is:
questions.solution: Path durations: 1-2-4 = 11, 1-3-4 = 11. The critical path is the longest = 11 days.

figure.caption: CPM activity-on-arrow network

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 5 min at a performance rating of 120%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 5×120% = 6 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vibration analysis — order tracking:
options: FFT to identify fault frequencies. 1× = imbalance, 2× = misalignment, bearing (BPFO, BPFI, BSF, FTF)
questions.solution: Vibration: envelope analysis for bearing defects. BPFO (outer race), BPFI (inner race). Order = f/f_ref.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inventory — pipeline (in-transit) stock:
options: Between locations. I_pipeline = (annual demand / 365) × transit days
questions.solution: Pipeline: order not yet received. Determined by lead time and demand rate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the control chart shown (CL = 60, UCL = 72, LCL = 48), the highlighted point lying above the UCL indicates that the process is:
options: Out of control (an assignable cause is present)
In control with only chance variation
questions.solution: A point beyond the ±3σ control limits (UCL = 72) signals an assignable cause — the process is out of statistical control.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Work study components:
options: Method study (improve method) + Work measurement (set time standards)
questions.solution: Work study: systematic examination of work methods. Improves productivity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: DMAIC — Control phase tools:
options: SPC, control plan, mistake-proofing, standard work, training
questions.solution: Control: sustain gains. Statistical process control. Process documentation.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An X̄ (X-bar) control chart is used to monitor the process:
questions.solution: The X̄ chart tracks the central tendency (mean) of a process over time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram categories (6M):
options: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature (Environment)
questions.solution: Fishbone: 6M categories. Brainstorming tool. Systematic cause identification.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Pareto chart of defect causes shown (tallest bar = cause C), the principle being applied says you should first address:
options: Cause C (the tallest bar — the vital few)
questions.solution: The Pareto (80/20) principle prioritizes the 'vital few' tallest bars; cause C contributes the most defects and is tackled first.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 240 140" width="240" height="140"><line x1="30" y1="120" x2="220" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="30" y1="15" x2="30" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><rect x="45" y="35" width="28" height="85" fill="#2563eb"/><text x="59" y="132" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">C</text><rect x="85" y="70" width="28" height="50" fill="#60a5fa"/><text x="99" y="132" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">P</text><rect x="125" y="92" width="28" height="28" fill="#93c5fd"/><text x="139" y="132" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">Q</text><rect x="165" y="105" width="28" height="15" fill="#bfdbfe"/><text x="179" y="132" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle">R</text><polyline points="59,40 99,60 139,85 179,100" fill="none" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1.5"/></svg>
figure.caption: Pareto chart of defects

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 80 units/day with a lead time of 10 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 80×10 = 800 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Safety stock is held primarily to protect against:
options: Uncertainty in demand and lead time
questions.solution: Safety (buffer) stock cushions against variability so stockouts are avoided.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Annual demand = 8000 units, ordering cost = ₹20/order, holding cost = ₹8/unit/yr. The EOQ is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: With demand 2000/yr, order cost ₹50 and order quantity 200, the annual ordering cost is:
questions.solution: Annual ordering cost = (D/Q)×S = (2000/200)×50 = ₹500.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: FAST diagram — critical path:
options: How? (left to right) and Why? (right to left). Basic function linked. Higher-order (left) vs lower-order (right)
questions.solution: FAST: Function Analysis System Technique. Bytheway method. Technical vs aesthetic functions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A product is used at 30 units/day with a lead time of 3 days. The reorder point (no safety stock) is:
questions.solution: ROP = demand rate × lead time = 30×3 = 90 units.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Economic batch quantity (EBQ) for production:
options: Q = sqrt(2DS/H * p/(p-d)) where p=production rate, d=demand rate
questions.solution: EBQ: finite replenishment. Factor p/(p-d) > 1 makes EBQ > EOQ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RULA (Rapid Upper Limb Assessment) score interpretation:
options: Score 1-2: acceptable. 3-4: investigate. 5-6: change soon. 7: change immediately
questions.solution: RULA: upper limb disorders. Score based on posture, muscle use, force.

questions.subject: Industrial Engineering
questions.stem: CRAFT (Computerized Relative Allocation of Facilities):
options: Improves layout by swapping department locations
questions.solution: CRAFT: iterative improvement. Swaps departments to reduce material handling cost. Uses from-to data.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Gantt chart vs network diagram:
options: Gantt: shows schedule, easy to read. Network: shows dependencies
questions.solution: Gantt: horizontal bars for tasks. Network (AON/AOA): precedence relationships.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Ishikawa (fishbone) diagram:
options: Cause-and-effect. 6M: Man, Machine, Material, Method, Measurement, Mother Nature
questions.solution: Fishbone: brainstorms root causes. Categorize by 6M. Drill down to find root cause.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement about Little's law is correct?
options: It holds for any stable queuing system regardless of distribution
It applies only to single-server queues
questions.solution: L = λW is distribution-free; it applies to any stable system in steady state.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hungarian method steps:
options: Row reduction, column reduction, line covering, augmentation
questions.solution: Hungarian: O(n³). Minimization. For maximization: negate costs or subtract from max.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: TRIZ (Theory of Inventive Problem Solving):
options: 40 inventive principles. Contradiction matrix. Technical and physical contradictions
questions.solution: TRIZ: Altshuller. System of engineering parameters. Separation principles for physical contradictions.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: CPM — Critical Path Method:
questions.solution: CPM: activity-on-node (AON). Critical path: longest path. Float: LS-ES or LF-EF.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Fixed position layout:
options: Product stays in place, resources move (shipbuilding, aircraft, construction)
questions.solution: Fixed: large/heavy product. Cranes, tools, workers brought to site.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bottleneck management (TOC):
questions.solution: TOC: throughput, inventory, operating expense. Drum-Buffer-Rope (DBR). Theory of Constraints.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Anthropometry: design for 5th percentile female:
options: Ensures 95% of female population can use (reaches, fits)
questions.solution: Design: reach → 5th percentile female. Strength → 5th percentile male. Clearance → 95th percentile.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A worker's observed time is 4 min at a performance rating of 80%. The normal time is:
questions.solution: Normal time = observed × rating = 4×80% = 3.2 min.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Linear programming: dual variable value y_i represents:
options: Shadow price of i-th constraint — change in optimal Z per unit increase in b_i
questions.solution: Dual: y_i = ∂Z*/∂b_i. Shadow price: value of relaxing one unit of constraint i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A PERT activity has to = 4 and tp = 14 days. Its standard deviation is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Critical chain project management (CCPM):
options: Remove safety from each activity → project buffer. Feeding buffers for non-critical paths. Goldratt's theory
questions.solution: CCMP: 50% duration estimates. Resource leveling first. Buffer management: green/yellow/red. Fever chart.
